engineGuide.h2: Nissan Pulsar Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Nissan Pulsar across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: Renault-sourced 1.5L dCi, entry-level diesel for Visia/Acenta, known for fuel economy but injector sensitivity.
compatibleVariants: Visia 1.5 dCi, Acenta 1.5 dCi, Acenta 1.5 dCi CVT, N-Connecta 1.5 dCi (pre-facelift)
commonFailures: Injector failure causing diesel knock (60k-90k miles typical)
EGR cooler clogging in urban stop-start use
DPF regeneration failure leading to backpressure damage
entries.cta: Get quotes for K9K 764 Nissan Pulsar engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: Updated K9K variant with revised ECU mapping, fitted to mid-spec N-Connecta and later Acenta models.
compatibleVariants: Acenta 1.5 dCi, N-Connecta 1.5 dCi, Tekna 1.5 dCi (pre-facelift)
commonFailures: EGR valve carbon buildup restricting airflow
Injector seal leaks causing oil dilution
entries.cta: Get quotes for K9K 782 Nissan Pulsar engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel (Euro 6)
entries.history: Euro 6-compliant K9K with updated DPF/AdBlue system, standard on facelift Tekna and N-Connecta.

entries.title: Connecta 1.5 dCi Euro 6, Tekna 1.5 dCi Euro 6, Acenta 1.5 dCi Euro 6
commonFailures: DPF soot overload from short-journey driving
AdBlue injector crystallisation causing NOx faults
entries.cta: Get quotes for K9K 788 Nissan Pulsar engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: Premium high-output diesel for Tekna trim, offering stronger torque and refined cruising for motorway use.

entries.title: pressure fuel pump wear (injector knock)

compatibleVariants: Visia 1.2 DIG-T, Acenta 1.2 DIG-T, N-Connecta 1.2 DIG-T, Tekna 1.2 DIG-T (all manual/CVT)
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure causing rattle on cold start
Turbocharger oil seal leaks (blue smoke under load)
Piston ring wear leading to excessive oil consumption

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: High-output turbo petrol exclusive to Nismo, delivering performance-car thrills with specialist rebuild requirements.
commonFailures: Turbocharger bearing wear at high boost pressures
Piston ring land cracking under hard driving

entries.title: pressure fuel pump failure causing lean running

commonProblems.h2: Nissan Pulsar Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: K9K Injector Failure - Why Pulsar 1.5 dCi Engines Develop Diesel Knock
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Pulsar Visia, Acenta, N-Connecta, Tekna 1.5 dCi (2014-2018, K9K 764/782/788)
problems.rootCause: High-pressure common rail injectors wear internally, causing poor atomisation, uneven combustion and the characteristic diesel knock.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace 1-2 faulty injectors, recode to ECU, fuel system clean
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; other injectors likely to follow within 12 months

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Full Injector Set + Fuel Pump
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all 4 injectors, high-pressure pump, rails, full calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine compression is good and no bottom-end damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt K9K with upgraded injectors, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage Pulsars; eliminates repeat injector risk

problems.recommendation: On a Pulsar worth £3,000-£6,000 with over 80k miles, a full rebuilt K9K engine with upgraded injectors is often more cost-effective than repeated injector repairs. We recommend getting a compression test first to rule out bottom-end damage.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt K9K engine prices for your Nissan Pulsar

problems.h4: HR12DDT Timing Chain Failure - Why Pulsar 1.2 DIG-T Engines Rattle on Cold Start
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Pulsar Visia, Acenta, N-Connecta, Tekna 1.2 DIG-T (2014-2018, HR12DDT)
problems.rootCause: The timing chain tensioner loses hydraulic pressure over time, allowing chain slack that causes rattling and can lead to jumped timing or valve damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, guides; reset ECU timing adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative fix if caught early before valve contact

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Cylinder Head Repair + Chain Kit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: If valves bent: head skim, new valves/seals, plus full timing kit
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if block compression is good and no piston damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt HR12DDT with upgraded tensioner, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for engines with valve damage or high mileage

problems.recommendation: If you hear a metallic rattle on cold start that disappears after 2-3 seconds, get the timing chain kit replaced preventatively. If the rattle is constant or accompanied by misfires, valve damage is likely and a rebuilt engine is the safer long-term investment.
problems.cta: Get quotes for HR12DDT Nissan Pulsar engine replacement

problems.h4: K9K DPF Regeneration Failure - Why Pulsar Diesels Lose Power in Urban Use
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Pulsar Acenta, N-Connecta, Tekna 1.5 dCi Euro 6 (2016-2018, K9K 788)
problems.rootCause: Frequent short journeys prevent active DPF regeneration; soot accumulates, increasing backpressure and eventually causing turbo strain or engine damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Off-car DPF clean, ECU forced regeneration, EGR clean
repairOptions.longevity: Only works if DPF is not physically damaged or melted

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New DPF unit, EGR valve clean/replace, full system reset
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine has no secondary damage from backpressure

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt K9K with Euro 6 DPF
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt K9K 788 with new DPF, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if backpressure has damaged turbo or bearings

problems.recommendation: If your Pulsar is used mainly for short urban trips, a DPF delete (where legal) or switching to a pre-Euro 6 K9K engine may be more practical than repeated DPF replacements. Always get a backpressure test before committing to DPF work.
problems.cta: Compare Euro 6 K9K engine prices for your Nissan Pulsar

problems.h4: R9M Turbo Actuator Failure - Why Pulsar 1.6 dCi Engines Enter Limp Mode
problems.rootCause: The variable nozzle turbo actuator sticks due to carbon buildup or electrical fault, preventing boost control and triggering limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace VNT actuator, recalibrate via diagnostic tool
repairOptions.longevity: Works if turbo internals are undamaged and vanes move freely

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip turbo, clean vanes, replace bearings/seals, new actuator
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if compressor/turbine wheels are undamaged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt R9M with balanced turbo, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage Tekna or if turbo damage is severe

problems.recommendation: On a Tekna worth £7,000+, a turbo rebuild is often cost-effective if caught early. If the turbo has seized or caused bearing damage, a full rebuilt R9M engine offers better long-term value and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Get quotes for R9M 412 Nissan Pulsar engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Nissan Pulsar
finalCta.paragraph: Whether it's injector knock on the 1.5 dCi, timing chain rattle on the 1.2 DIG-T, or turbo issues on the 1.6 dCi, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Nissan Pulsar specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Nissan Pulsar Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair returns from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Nissan Pulsar and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Nissan Pulsar Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant

items.title: Nissan Pulsar 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L dCi K9K diesel was the backbone of the Pulsar range, fitted to Visia, Acenta, N-Connecta and early Tekna models. Renault-sourced for efficiency, it delivers strong low-end torque but requires careful maintenance of the fuel and emissions systems to avoid costly failures.
commonFailurePoints: DPF regeneration issues on Euro 6 variants
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan Pulsar 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Nissan Pulsar 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6L dCi R9M diesel was reserved for the premium Tekna trim from 2016 onwards, offering refined cruising and stronger motorway performance. Euro 6 compliant with AdBlue, it demands specialist calibration during replacement to ensure emissions system compatibility.
commonFailurePoints: timing chain tensioner wear at higher mileage
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan Pulsar 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Nissan Pulsar 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: Nissan's 1.2L DIG-T HR12DDT petrol turbo balanced efficiency and punch across most Pulsar trims. The timing chain tensioner is the critical wear item; rebuilt units from our network include upgraded tensioners and guides to prevent repeat failures.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner failure causing cold-start rattle
piston ring wear leading to oil consumption
items.cta: Compare Nissan Pulsar 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Nissan Pulsar 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L DIG-T MR16DDT was exclusive to the performance-oriented Nismo variant, delivering 218hp with a sport-tuned turbo and reinforced internals. Specialist rebuilds are essential due to the high-stress operating environment and limited donor availability. Not sure which engine size your Nissan Pulsar needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Nissan Pulsar specialists.
commonFailurePoints: Turbo bearing wear under high boost

engineYears.h2: Nissan Pulsar Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Nissan Pulsar's 2014-2018 production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Pulsar launched with K9K 1.5L diesel and HR12DDT 1.2L petrol engines, establishing the core powertrains for the range.
keyChanges: N17 chassis introduced as C-segment hatchback replacement for Tiida
K9K 764/782 1.5L dCi diesel standard on Visia, Acenta, N-Connecta
HR12DDT 1.2L DIG-T petrol turbo available across all trims
CVT automatic option introduced for select petrol/diesel variants
knownFor: Strong initial sales driven by competitive pricing and equipment
Early K9K injector failures emerging in high-mileage fleet use
HR12DDT timing chain rattle reports appearing around 70k miles
Good parts availability due to Renault K9K cross-compatibility
years.cta: Get quotes for 2014-2016 Nissan Pulsar replacement engines
years.ticker: Pulsar 1.5 dCi injector knock • Pulsar 1.2 DIG-T timing chain rattle • Pulsar engine replacement cost UK

years.description: The facelift introduced Euro 6 emissions compliance, updating K9K to 788 spec and adding the R9M 1.6L diesel for Tekna.
keyChanges: Euro 6 emissions standard mandatory for new registrations from Sept 2016
K9K 788 introduced with updated DPF/AdBlue system for Euro 6 compliance
R9M 412 1.6L dCi added as premium diesel option for Tekna trim
Minor exterior refresh and infotainment updates across range
mainEngines: K9K 788 - 1.5L diesel Euro 6 (Acenta, N-Connecta, Tekna facelift)
R9M 412 - 1.6L diesel Euro 6 (Tekna premium variant only)
HR12DDT - 1.2L petrol turbo (continued across all trims)
knownFor: Euro 6 DPF regeneration issues in urban stop-start driving
R9M turbo actuator failures emerging on high-mileage Tekna models
HR12DDT oil consumption reports increasing on post-100k mile engines
Strong residual values for well-maintained facelift examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 2016-2018 Nissan Pulsar replacement engines
years.ticker: Pulsar Euro 6 DPF warning • Pulsar 1.6 dCi turbo limp mode • Pulsar facelift engine compatibility

years.description: The limited-run Nismo variant brought the MR16DDT 1.6L high-output turbo petrol, requiring specialist rebuild expertise.
keyChanges: Nismo performance trim launched with exclusive MR16DDT engine
Sport-tuned suspension, body kit and interior upgrades
MR16DDT features reinforced internals and high-pressure turbo
Limited production run; high specialist demand for replacements
knownFor: Enthusiast appeal with 218hp and sporty handling
Turbo bearing wear under hard driving or poor maintenance
Limited donor availability driving up rebuilt engine costs
Specialist calibration required for ECU and boost control
years.cta: Enquire about Nissan Pulsar Nismo MR16DDT engine availability

faq.h2: Nissan Pulsar Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Nissan Pulsar engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt engine prices for the Nissan Pulsar range from £1,200 for a 1.5L K9K diesel to £4,500 for the Nismo MR16DDT, depending on engine code, variant and condition type. Supply-only pricing; fitting extra.
keyPoints: K9K 1.5L diesel rebuilt: £1,200-£2,400 supply only
HR12DDT 1.2L petrol rebuilt: £1,400-£2,600 supply only
R9M 1.6L diesel premium: £1,800-£3,200 supply only
MR16DDT Nismo specialist: £2,500-£4,500 supply only
Fitting typically adds £400-£900 depending on garage
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Nissan Pulsar engine prices now

items.question: What engine codes fit my Nissan Pulsar?
items.answer: Your Pulsar's engine code depends on variant and year: K9K 764/782/788 for 1.5L diesel, R9M 412 for 1.6L diesel Tekna, HR12DDT for 1.2L petrol, or MR16DDT for Nismo. Enter your reg to confirm exact fitment.
keyPoints: K9K series: 1.5L dCi diesel, Visia/Acenta/N-Connecta/Tekna
R9M 412: 1.6L dCi diesel, Tekna facelift only
HR12DDT: 1.2L DIG-T petrol, all trims manual/CVT
MR16DDT: 1.6L DIG-T petrol, Nismo performance only
DVLA lookup via registration confirms exact code instantly
items.cta: Check your Nissan Pulsar engine code compatibility now

items.question: How long does Nissan Pulsar engine replacement take?
items.answer: Most Nissan Pulsar engine replacements are completed within 2-3 working days once the engine is sourced, with fitting taking 1-2 days depending on garage workload and ancillary checks required.
keyPoints: Engine sourcing: 24-48 hours for common K9K/HR12DDT codes
Fitting labour: 8-14 hours typical for Pulsar engine swap
ECU calibration: Additional 1-2 hours for Euro 6 variants
Total turnaround: 2-3 days typical from quote to collection
items.cta: Book Nissan Pulsar engine supply & fit with fast turnaround

items.question: Do Nissan Pulsar replacement engines come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every rebuilt, reconditioned or remanufactured Nissan Pulsar engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists; used engines typically carry 3-6 months.
keyPoints: Rebuilt/recon engines: 12-month unlimited mileage standard
Used engines: 3-6 months typical, confirm with supplier
Warranty covers mechanical failure, not misuse or installation error
Keep all invoices and service records for warranty validation
items.cta: Get Nissan Pulsar engine quotes with 12-month warranty included

items.question: Can I supply my own Nissan Pulsar engine for fitting?
items.answer: Yes, many garages in our network will fit customer-sourced Nissan Pulsar engines, but warranty validity may be affected and we recommend using our vetted suppliers for full coverage and compatibility assurance.
keyPoints: Supply & fit quotes available with engine included or labour-only
Customer-sourced engines require compression test proof
Warranty may be voided if non-approved engine is fitted
ECU calibration must be confirmed compatible before fitting
items.cta: Compare Nissan Pulsar supply & fit deals with warranty protection

items.question: What's the difference between rebuilt, reconditioned, and used Pulsar engines?
items.answer: Used Pulsar engines are low-mileage donor units as-removed; reconditioned engines are cleaned and tested with worn parts replaced; rebuilt engines undergo comprehensive strip-down with upgraded components addressing known failure points.
keyPoints: Used: Budget option, verify mileage and compression history
Reconditioned: Balanced cost/reliability, worn parts replaced
Rebuilt: Best long-term value, upgraded injectors/timing kits included
All types available through our network; compare quotes to decide
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs reconditioned Nissan Pulsar engine options

items.question: Will a replacement engine affect my Nissan Pulsar insurance?
items.answer: A like-for-like Nissan Pulsar engine replacement typically does not affect insurance premiums, but you must declare the engine change to your insurer; performance upgrades or non-standard codes may impact cover.
keyPoints: Declare engine replacement to insurer to maintain policy validity
Like-for-like code swap rarely affects premium calculations
Performance upgrades (e.g., MR16DDT) may require specialist cover
Keep replacement invoice and engine number for DVLA update
items.cta: Get Nissan Pulsar engine quotes with documentation support

items.question: How do I know if my Nissan Pulsar needs an engine replacement or just a repair?
items.answer: If your Pulsar has severe knocking, loss of compression, or catastrophic failure symptoms, replacement is likely more cost-effective; for isolated issues like a single injector, repair may suffice-get a free diagnostic checklist via our quote form.
keyPoints: Compression test: Below 100psi on any cylinder suggests replacement
Metallic knocking under load often indicates bottom-end damage
Single injector failure may be repairable if compression is good
Free diagnostic checklist available when requesting quotes
items.cta: Download free Nissan Pulsar repair vs replace decision guide

items.question: Can I upgrade my Nissan Pulsar engine during replacement?
items.answer: Engine upgrades on the Nissan Pulsar (e.g., K9K to R9M) require ECU reprogramming, potential transmission compatibility checks, and may affect insurance; we recommend like-for-like replacements for warranty and compliance simplicity.
keyPoints: Like-for-like code swap ensures ECU and emissions compliance
Cross-code swaps require specialist calibration and may void warranty
Performance upgrades may impact insurance group and MOT emissions
Consult our specialists before considering any engine upgrade path
items.cta: Speak to a Nissan Pulsar engine specialist about upgrade options

items.question: What documentation do I need for Nissan Pulsar engine replacement?
items.answer: You'll need your V5C registration document, engine replacement invoice, and the new engine's number/certificate to update the DVLA; MOT certificate may be required if emissions-related work was performed.
keyPoints: V5C required to update engine number with DVLA post-replacement
Keep supplier invoice showing engine code and warranty terms
New engine certificate helps verify emissions compliance for MOT
DVLA update is free and can be completed online or by post
items.cta: Get Nissan Pulsar engine quotes with full documentation support
